channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uhuru Attends Tana Forum in Ghana to Discuss Peace and Security in Africa
item.description: Former President Uhuru Kenyatta travelled to Accra, Ghana, to attend the Tana Forum on Security in Africa, a significant platform where African leaders, policymakers, and security experts gather to di...

item.title: PM Shehbaz's daughter and son-in-law granted interim bail in FIA money laundering case
item.description: On Tuesday, a special central court in Lahore granted interim pre-arrest bail to Rabia Imran, daughter of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, and her husband Imran Yousaf in connection with a money launder...

item.title: U.S. Government to Compensate Companies for Canceling Offshore Wind Projects and Shifting to Fossil Fuels
item.description: The Trump administration has announced agreements to reimburse energy companies a total of $885 million to cancel two planned offshore wind farm projects in the United States. In exchange, the compani...

item.title: 92-year-old man charged with murder following the death of an elderly woman in Exeter
item.description: Devon and Cornwall Police have charged a 92-year-old man with murder after the death of an 87-year-old woman in Exeter. Authorities were called to a property on Millbrook Lane on 19 April following co...

item.title: Senator Lacson to Present Flood Control Committee Findings Despite Incomplete Signatures
item.description: Eight months after the Senate blue ribbon committee began investigating alleged corruption in flood control projects, the committee report has not yet received the required signatures for official fil...

item.title: US Foreign Policy Failures and Lessons from History
item.description: The US remains the world’s foremost military and economic power, but its ongoing difficulties in Iran highlight a failure to learn from history. Despite immense resources and advanced weaponry, Americ...

item.title: Venezuela Oil Production Still Low as Majority of Wells Remain Inactive
item.description: Venezuela oil sector still dey struggle well-well as less than 30 percent of the country oil wells dey currently active, according to Venezuelan Petroleum Chamber (CPV). Out of over 30,000 oil wells w...

item.title: Hazariibag Man Drowns in Lake Fishing Incident
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Hazariibag, India, when a man lost his life while fishing in a local lake. Details surrounding the exact circumstances of the drowning are still emerging, but authorities...

item.title: Nyeri: Authorities Crack Down on Illegal Alcohol Production, Hunt for MCA Aspirant
item.description: A major operation in Nyeri County has uncovered a sophisticated, illegal alcohol production and bottling setup hidden within a residential home. The National Authority for the Campaign Against Alcohol...

item.title: South Africa Celebrates 32 Years of Democracy with Nationwide Freedom Day Events
item.description: South Africans across the country gathered on 27 April 2026 to commemorate Freedom Day, marking 32 years since the nation’s first democratic elections in 1994. The main national event took place at th...

item.title: Santa Marta Conference Pushes for Global Phaseout of Fossil Fuels Amid Iran War Energy Crisis
item.description: A new international conference in Santa Marta, Colombia, aims to create a practical roadmap for transitioning away from fossil fuels. The First International Conference on the Transition Away From Fos...

item.title: Leavitt's Criticism of Trump Comparisons Backfires as VP Vance's Past Remarks Surface
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt recently attempted to deflect criticism of President Donald Trump by accusing left-wing groups of inciting political violence through comparisons of Trump ...

item.title: Rajnath Singh urges SCO to take a unified stand against terrorism and extremism
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh addressed the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers' meeting in Bishkek, where he stressed that terrorism remains the greatest threat to global peace...

item.title: Tanzania Focuses on Adding Value to Critical Mineral Resources
item.description: Mambo ni mazuri! Tanzania is really getting serious about benefitting from its minerals, especially these critical ones like lithium, cobalt, and nickel – the kinda stuff needed for clean energy. Depu...

item.title: Photographer Ketaki Sheth’s Candid Glimpses of Bollywood Sets in the 1980s and 1990s
item.description: Ketaki Sheth’s latest collection, 'Flashback', offers a rare behind-the-scenes glimpse into Bollywood and Tamil film sets between 1985 and 1993. Featuring 57 black-and-white photographs, the series ca...

item.title: Ramaphosa Urges South Africans to Reject Xenophobia and Remember Pan-African Solidarity
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has called on South Africans to reflect on the support the country received from other African nations during the anti-apartheid struggle, urging citizens to reject xenophobi...

item.title: Protecting Local News is Vital for a Healthy Democracy
item.description: The Nightly editorial underscores the crucial role of local news in maintaining a robust Australian democracy. Over decades, trusted news organisations have cultivated strong relationships with their ...

item.title: Okeng'o Nyambane Gaining Ground in Nyaribari Chache MP Race After Elders' Support
item.description: Support for Okeng'o Nyambane’s bid to become the next Member of Parliament for Nyaribari Chache is rapidly growing, following a powerful endorsement from respected elders of the Botondo clan. During a...

item.title: Babar Azam Recovers Form Through Tactical 'Unlearning' in PSL
item.description: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam seems to have rediscovered his batting mojo this Pakistan Super League (PSL) season. After a period where his form dipped, the star batsman has returned to the crease...

item.title: Health Risks of Unregulated Sexual Enhancement Pills in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, the use of unregulated sexual enhancement pills has led to serious health complications, highlighting the risks associated with buying cheap products from informal markets. Sizwe Mthe...
